title: Allstate names the most collision-prone cities in the U.S.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston was ranked the most collision-prone city in the U.S. with drivers averaging 3.7 years between crashes.
Worcester and Springfield, MA, rank 197th and 196th, respectively, in Allstate's 2026 report on collision-prone cities.
Providence, RI, is among the top 10 riskiest cities with an average of 5.87 years between collisions.

title: Falmouth man, 74, arrested after police say he led troopers on wrong-way, high-speed chase over Bourne Bridge
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Armando Deleon, 74, was arrested after a 105 mph wrong-way chase over Bourne Bridge, leading to numerous charges.
The chase began after Deleon's vehicle nearly hit emergency responders on Route 25 in Bourne, escalating into a high-speed pursuit.
Deleon faces charges including obstructing an emergency vehicle and assault with a dangerous weapon, and is held without bail.

title: Boston police release new photos of alleged lemonade stand robber
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Boston police seek public help to identify a juvenile suspect in a South Boston lemonade stand robbery.
Surveillance photos show the suspect wearing black attire during the June 10 incident on West Ninth Street.
A 14-year-old was previously arrested for the robbery and charged in Boston Juvenile Court with armed robbery.

title: Jayson Tatum and Brad Stevens appear on Rich Paul’s big board of LeBron James landing spots
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Celtics' Tatum and Stevens noted as potential landing spot for LeBron James by his agent Rich Paul.
James is leaving the Lakers after eight seasons, exploring options for a final NBA team.
Philadelphia, Miami, Denver, and Minnesota are top destinations, with Boston seen as a less likely option.

title: Robert Parish not a fan of Celtics’ decision to trade Jaylen Brown: ‘If Red [Auerbach] was alive … I think this would have made him nauseated’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Robert Parish criticized the Celtics' decision to trade Jaylen Brown to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Parish compared the Brown trade to the controversial Luka Doncic deal by the Mavericks.
He speculated that former Celtics coach Red Auerbach would have disapproved of the trade.

title: Jayson Tatum bids farewell to Jaylen Brown: ‘Nothing but love and respect for you’
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum wrote a farewell message to Jaylen Brown after Brown's trade to the Philadelphia 76ers.
Jaylen Brown was traded to the 76ers in exchange for Paul George and multiple draft picks.
Brown expressed feeling disrespected by the Celtics' handling of the trade process.

title: Celtics, Neemias Queta reportedly agree to four-year extension
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Celtics and Neemias Queta agree on a four-year, $56 million extension through the 2030-31 season.
Queta, a seven-foot center, averaged 10.2 points and 8.4 rebounds in 76 games for the Celtics last season.
Originally drafted by the Kings in 2021, Queta signed with the Celtics in 2023 and has steadily improved since.

title: Union Oyster House turns 200: Inside America’s oldest continuously operating restaurant
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston celebrates 200 years as America's oldest continuously operating restaurant, originally established in 1826.
The restaurant, a National Historic Landmark, was designated in 2003; its building dates back to the early 1700s.
Union Oyster House serves 1,000 to 1,500 guests daily; their clam chowder remains a popular menu item since 1834.

title: Boston’s biggest summer events collide over the next 8 days. Buckle up.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston hosts major summer events: Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular, World Cup quarterfinal, and Sail Boston's Parade of Sail.
Boston Pops Fireworks on July 4 features performances by Chance the Rapper and a revolutionary-themed drone show.
World Cup quarterfinal at Gillette Stadium and Sail Boston's Parade of Sail occur between July 9 and July 11.

title: The presidents, celebrities, and famous regulars of Boston’s Union Oyster House
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: Union Oyster House in Boston has hosted six presidents, including JFK and Barack Obama.
A 2003 painting by Stan Kotzen at Union Oyster House depicts over 50 iconic celebrity guests.
Notable visitors include Al Pacino, Meryl Streep, Clint Eastwood, and Robert Redford.
